_doc: §8.4a.21 W3 Bedrock shelf. Authoritative-source lookup for S2 source discovery. Per-domain list of organizations whose primary publications the LLM should weight heavily over blog posts / opinion / unverified web content. Curation pass: Campbell + Opus joint at W3.
_revision: v0.2 — Campbell + Opus joint curation pass landed 2026-05-17 (§8.4a.21 W3 RATIFIED). DSCA disposition: keep with scope note demoting strategic-framing weight. Christensen disposition (analog_seed_bibliography.json): keep with contested flag, no mandatory_disclosure (Campbell 2026-05-17). Optional `scope` field added per Bedrock entry, used on DSCA for v0.2.

Economics.description: Finance, economics, accounting, capital markets. Used for module topics like quarterly earnings statements, monetary policy, debt structures, etc.

sources.name: SEC
sources.notes: Primary source for filings (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K), staff guidance, enforcement actions.

sources.name: FASB
sources.notes: Authoritative for U.S. GAAP.

sources.name: CFA Institute
sources.notes: Curriculum + research publications; framework definitions.

sources.name: IFRS
sources.notes: International accounting standards (non-U.S. comparator to FASB).

sources.name: FRED
sources.notes: Time-series macroeconomic data from the St. Louis Fed.

sources.name: BEA
sources.notes: Industry economic accounts; satellite accounts including the space economy.

sources.name: BLS
sources.notes: Wages / employment / productivity statistics.

sources.name: NBER
sources.notes: Peer-reviewed economics working papers.

sources.name: OECD
sources.full_name: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
sources.notes: International comparisons; OECD Space Economy reports specifically.

sources.name: IMF
sources.notes: Macroeconomic data + working papers; country surveillance reports.

sources.name: World Bank
sources.notes: Development-economics framing; country economic data.

sources.name: RAND
sources.notes: Defense and economics research. Cross-listed under Policy + Technology.

Policy.description: Defense policy, national security, space policy, regulatory frameworks.

sources.name: DoD doctrine
sources.full_name: U.S. Department of Defense — Joint Doctrine + Joint Publications
sources.notes: Joint Publications (JP 3-14 Space Operations, JP 3-12 Cyberspace, etc.) are doctrinal source-of-truth for U.S. military space concepts.

sources.name: GAO
sources.notes: Audit + evaluation reports on defense/space programs; widely cited.

sources.name: RAND
sources.notes: Defense-focused FFRDC research; Project Air Force, NDRI.

sources.name: NSSP
sources.full_name: U.S. National Space Strategy / National Space Policy documents
sources.notes: Executive-level space policy directives (SPDs, national strategy docs).

sources.name: CRS
sources.notes: Non-partisan analysis for Congress; excellent backgrounders on defense/space topics.

sources.name: State PM Affairs
sources.full_name: U.S. State Department — Bureau of Political-Military Affairs
sources.notes: Partner-country engagement + security-assistance policy.

sources.name: USSF/USSPACECOM doctrine
sources.full_name: U.S. Space Force + U.S. Space Command — Service-Specific Doctrine and Strategic Guidance
sources.notes: Service-specific doctrine + strategy distinct from joint DoD doctrine.

sources.name: Aerospace Corporation
sources.notes: Federally funded R&D center; technical-policy bridge documents; Center for Space Policy & Strategy.

sources.name: CSIS Aerospace Security Project
sources.notes: Heavily cited, methodologically transparent space-security analysis.

sources.name: NDU Press
sources.full_name: National Defense University Press — Joint Forces Quarterly, Strategic Studies Quarterly, Air & Space Power Journal (now Æther)
sources.notes: Peer-reviewed military-academic journals.

sources.name: DSCA
sources.scope: procedural / technical-process detail only; not strategic-framing
sources.notes: Authoritative for FMS procedural / regulatory / technical-process detail (DSCA 5105.38-M / SAMM, etc.). NOT a strategic-framing source — DSCA is often cited as exemplifying what is wrong with FMS (risk-averse, process-bound, solves all problems by adding process). Use for 'how an FMS case progresses procedurally' questions; do not use for 'what is wrong with FMS as an institution' framing. Listed last in Policy to reflect ranking demotion.

Technology.description: Space industry technical standards, engineering, technology development.

sources.name: AIAA
sources.full_name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
sources.notes: Peer-reviewed journals + conference papers in aerospace engineering.

sources.name: ITU
sources.notes: Spectrum + orbital slot allocation authority; ITU-R recommendations.

sources.name: FCC
sources.notes: U.S. satellite licensing + spectrum allocation; rule-makings and orders.

sources.name: NASA technical reports
sources.notes: Engineering papers, test reports, mission documentation from NASA programs.

sources.name: IEEE
sources.full_name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
sources.notes: Peer-reviewed engineering literature broadly; standards for communications + signal processing.

sources.name: ESA technical reports
sources.full_name: European Space Agency — Technical Reports and Publications
sources.notes: European equivalent of NASA NTRS; mission docs + technical bulletins.

sources.name: NIST
sources.full_name: U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology
sources.notes: Cybersecurity standards relevant for space systems (SP 800 series, FIPS).

sources.name: USSF SDA
sources.full_name: U.S. Space Force — Space Development Agency Public Documentation
sources.notes: Live programmatic source for SDA tranches (PWSA Transport + Tracking layers).

sources.name: IADC
sources.notes: Debris / sustainability coordination across major space agencies.

sources.name: COSPAR
sources.notes: International space-research coordination; planetary protection guidelines.

sources.name: RAND
sources.notes: Defense + technology research. Cross-listed under Policy + Economics.

Cross-cutting.description: Sources that span multiple domains (e.g. CRS reports on space economics; RAND on policy + technology).
Cross-cutting._curation_note: Populate as actual cross-cutting needs emerge. RAND now appears explicitly in all three primary domains (Economics, Policy, Technology); CRS commonly cross-references space-economic and space-policy material.

deny_list.description: URL patterns to explicitly exclude from S2 source discovery — blogs, opinion sections, low-trust content farms.
deny_list._curation_note: Expand cautiously — some substack writers are domain experts. Better to soft-rank than hard-exclude.
